programmes.show_description: Jay gives Ieuan a tribute to his late sister, Sketch evicts the eye on Mike's thigh, Glen tackles Ben's boastful ink, and Alice decorates Abbie's drunken doodle.
Alex Horne is pelted with milk and gin, Bridget Christie has many questions about shoes, Judi Love is bewildered by bunting, and Ardal O'Hanlon looks a bit bereft.
Jimmy Carr hosts, as Sarah Millican and Nish Kumar take on Jon Richardson and Maisie Adam. David O'Doherty joins Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner. With Rachel Riley.
Teleshopping
Teleshopping
Sam Dushey, the CEO of Shoppers World, goes incognito among his own workforce, seeking reassurance that his stores are performing to his high standards as the business grows.
Tim Betts, the CEO of Muscle Maker Grill, goes undercover in the restaurant chain that he recently bought. Is every branch following the same high standards?
...Selling: Charlie, Renee and their daughters love cooking. So, a dream kitchen is at the top of their wish list. Can Drew and Jonathan add some flavour to their current place?
In Plymouth, Christine and Kevin serve a menu that even Christine isn't convinced about. Harvey and Natasha throw a Spanish fiesta. And will nerves get the better of Paul and Jo?
In south London, there are evil glares, a rooftop rave and a cooking meltdown on the hottest day of the year, as three couples compete to host the best dinner party.
The One with the Wannabe DJ Groom: Bride Ellis has dreamed her whole life of a quiet church wedding. Luke decides to plan a rave-inspired do...
The One on Top of a Mountain: Richard's gambling everything on a Welsh mountain-top wedding for his bride Jody, but even his best man is unsure about his plans.
Will it be child's play for Sharon John, CEO of Build-A-Bear Workshop, as she works undercover alongside the employees who custom-make stuffed toy animals in her retail outlets?
Karen Freeman-Wilson, the mayor of Gary in Indiana, goes incognito among the city's workforce and meets a sanitation plant operator, a firefighter and a police officer.
Flamangos: Gordon visits Flamangos in rural New Jersey, where mother and daughter Adele and Cheryl are stuck in their tropical ways. But their restaurant is far from paradise.
Bazzini: Award-winning chef Paul runs Italian joint Bazzini in New Jersey, but didn't realise how difficult running a restaurant would be. Can Gordon turn it around?
...and Selling: Brand new series: After buying their home at a top market price, Michelle and Danielle don't want to lose money. Will a huge renovation secure a high-selling sum?
Murder on the High Seas: Ben's mistake leaves everyone stranded at night in the middle of a storm. A new deckhand draws attention. And Fraser shares a kiss.
The comics are baffled by high fives and a fish tank. Bridget Christie has a present for the Taskmaster, and Sophie Duker covers some keys in saliva.
Jimmy Carr hosts as Lee Mack and Joe Wilkinson take on Jon Richardson and Jessica Knappett. Comedian Nigel Ng joins Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner. With Rachel Riley.
Jimmy Carr hosts the comedic mash-up. Jack Dee and Tom Allen take on Jon Richardson and Judi Love. Dr John Cooper Clarke joins Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner. With Rachel Riley.
The Affair: Mark discovers Jeremy's girlfriend Elena has a guilty secret, but should Mark tell Jez about this when he seems so happy? Elena doesn't think it's such a good idea!
The Party: As a last desperate hurrah before the baby arrives, Mark and Jeremy decide to throw a party.
Sketch de-thrones self-proclaimed king Dan. Alice cooks up a treat for ex-chef Melody. Jay helps Az make a wise choice as he moves on from his problematic past.
The comics are baffled by high fives and a fish tank. Bridget Christie has a present for the Taskmaster, and Sophie Duker covers some keys in saliva.
Jimmy Carr hosts as Lee Mack and Joe Wilkinson take on Jon Richardson and Jessica Knappett. Comedian Nigel Ng joins Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner. With Rachel Riley.
Teleshopping
Teleshopping
Jeff Young, the co-owner of custom signs company YESCO, goes undercover in his own business. He meets staff who have suffered misfortune but remain dedicated to their jobs.
Scott Gerber, the CEO of hospitality business the Gerber Group, goes undercover in his own organisation, to ensure it is maintaining the reputation that made it such a success.
...Selling: Jonathan and Drew come up with a way to dazzle Dennis and Marie's current home - including an unorthodox way of getting rid of an armchair...
On Guernsey and Alderney, Sophie and Turk show off the local produce, Dudley and Karla serve an authentic South African feast, and Bobbie and Jamie feel the pressure.
In the West Country, Charlotte and Miguel's Venezuelan menu competes with Pansy and Rob's sophisticated fare and Elle and Dave's organic, wheat-free feast.
The One with the Bride on Skates: Jaydene would love to get married in the same church her parents tied the knot in. Cue groom Luke, and his rollerblading wedding.
The Pirate Adventure One: Mathew's got some grand ideas for a swashbuckling pirate adventure-themed wedding to bride Jamie-Lee.
Greg Scott, the CEO of clothing retailer New York & Company, goes undercover among his own employees, seeking ways to keep the business in vogue and increase profitability.
Renee Maloney, the co-founder of art class studio business Painting with a Twist, goes undercover in her organisation to make sure that her projections of growth are possible.
Mojito: Gordon Ramsay visits a Cuban café in Brooklyn, where owners Marcelo and Kata fight over everything, driving away customers with their high-volume bickering.
PJ's Steakhouse: In New York, Gordon Ramsay visits PJ's Steakhouse, where owners Joe and Madalyn are in serious debt and struggling to keep the high-end restaurant afloat.
...and Selling: Brand new series: The twins tackle terrible tiles and when Danielle and Steve go house hunting, one home causes instant tears. But are they happy ones or not?
The Real Housewives of Grenada: Chef Anthony takes on his biggest challenge, while the relentless demands of a famous guest push the interior to their limit.
Ardal O'Hanlon straddles a mannequin, Chris Ramsey takes a sausage to the nose, Sophie Duker asks a very good question, and Alex Horne displays a mastery of mimicry.
Jimmy Carr hosts the comedy game show mash-up. Roisin Conaty and Kiri Pritchard-McLean take on Jon Richardson and Rob Beckett. With Huge Davies, Susie Dent and Rachel Riley.
Jimmy Carr hosts as Richard Ayoade and Sara Pascoe take on Jon Richardson and Chris McCausland. Morgana Robinson joins Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner. With Rachel Riley.
Das Boot: Elena and Gail's wedding is imminent, but Jeremy is busy plotting how to get rid of Gail and win Elena back.
Man Jam: Dobby is going out with Simon, a graphic designer. Mark and Gerrard form the 'Dobby Club', devoted to winning her over. But who will ultimately triumph, Mark or Gerrard?
